title: Retro Biosciences raises at $1.8B valuation — first private longevity unicorn outside reprogramming
description: Sam Altman-backed Retro Biosciences announced Friday it raised new capital at a $1.8B post-money valuation, marking one of the largest private longevity rounds. The company is running its first clinical trial — an oral pill enhancing protein-aggregate clearance in Alzheimer's patients — with CEO Joe Betts-LaCroix flagging no dose-limiting toxicities and initial data expected around August.

title: Rapamycin headwinds: New Zealand trial flags reduced exercise performance gains
description: RAPA-EX-01, published in the Journal of Cachexia, Sarcopenia and Muscle, shows that older adults on weekly rapamycin gained less from exercise than placebo — fewer chair-stand reps, ~16 fewer feet on 6-minute walk, 2.6 lb less grip force. The result complicates the rapamycin protocol stack and sharpens the rationale for UT San Antonio and U of Arizona Phase 3 dose-finding work.

title: NG101 first MRI-imaging human evidence of structural CNS rescue
description: Nature Communications now hosts the NervGen NG101 Phase 2b SCI data showing faster lesion volume reduction and slower decline of spinal cord area and myelin-sensitive MRI measures vs. placebo. The first MRI-based human evidence that a small molecule slows neurodegeneration and may promote fiber sprouting, with direct read-throughs to age-related CNS programs.

title: SIRT1 cemented as the cross-tissue exercise-mimetic mediator
description: A new Biogerontology long-term-exercise review codifies cross-species evidence that exercise raises SIRT1 mRNA, activity, and protein in muscle, brain, heart, and adipose tissues — proposing SIRT1 as the consistent mediator of exercise-driven healthspan benefits. Sharpens the case for SIRT1-activating compounds and feeds the geroscience policy stack outlined this week in Aging-US.

title: Lancet Healthy Longevity meta-analysis (145,465 participants) confirms exercise lowers epigenetic age
description: Across 44 studies and 145,465 participants, higher physical activity correlated with lower Horvath and GrimAge epigenetic age acceleration (not Hannum or PhenoAge). The largest-ever synthesis validates exercise as a population-scale geroprotector and confirms that clock methodology matters for measuring intervention response.

title: Retro Biosciences raises at $1.8 billion valuation
description: Sam Altman-backed Retro Biosciences announced new capital at $1.8B valuation; running first clinical trial — an oral pill enhancing protein-aggregate clearance in Alzheimer's. CEO Joe Betts-LaCroix says no dose-limiting toxicities seen, initial data expected around August.

title: Rapamycin and Exercise: New Study Finds Potential Performance Decline
description: RAPA-EX-01 RCT: older adults on weekly rapamycin completed ~2 fewer chair-stand reps, walked ~16 feet less, produced ~2.6 lb less grip force vs. placebo; CRP elevated in rapamycin group. Rapamycin did not affect biological age.

title: Long-Term Exercise Changes on Sirtuin 1 Signaling in Aged Tissues
description: Review links exercise-induced changes in SIRT1 signaling across tissues and species to improved metabolism, healthspan, and potential longevity benefits during aging.

title: NG101 and Structural Recovery in Human Spinal Cord Injury
description: NervGen NG101 Phase 2b participants showed faster lesion volume reduction and slower structural degeneration on MRI biomarkers vs. placebo — first small-molecule structural CNS rescue in chronic human SCI.

title: Short-Term Dietary Intervention Alters Physiological Profiles Relevant to Ageing
description: 4-week diet intervention in 104 adults 65-75 changed KDM biological age estimates, with several diet groups (especially omnivorous/high-carbohydrate) showing reduced deltaAge vs. high-fat controls.

title: Intestinal Mucosal Immune Aging and the Gut Microbiota
description: In aged mice, intestinal aging was associated with barrier dysfunction, immune imbalance, altered M cells, inflammatory bacterial enrichment, and reduced fecal IgA.

title: Retinal Myeloid Cells and TREM2 in Age-Related Macular Degeneration
description: Review summarizes how retinal microglia and macrophage-related TREM2 signaling influence dry and neovascular AMD pathology and may be therapeutically targetable.

title: Cellular senescence: from pathogenic mechanisms to precision anti-aging interventions
description: Review argues senescent cells are functionally heterogeneous and that future senolytic strategies should move toward precision clearance based on pathogenicity rather than marker presence alone.

title: Platelet-specific HuR controls inflammatory factors in systemic aging
description: Platelet-specific deletion of HuR reduced platelet-secreted pro-inflammatory factors, lowered tissue infiltration, and delayed systemic aging in aged mice — identifying platelets as a druggable inflammaging node.

title: LPS Priming Restores Wound Repair in Aged Mouse Skin
description: A short pre-injury LPS pulse accelerated skin repair in aged mice by boosting immune activity, epithelial regeneration, and barrier restoration.

title: Older Mice Offer New Insight Into Cancer and Aging
description: Melanoma spread in mice peaked in middle age and was lower in young and very old animals, with gamma delta T cells implicated in controlling metastasis — relevant to age-appropriate cancer models.

title: Role of Aging on Gamma Delta T Cells in Cutaneous Melanoma Progression
description: PROS1 from melanoma cells reduced gamma delta T cell infiltration in middle-aged mice and promoted aggressive lung and liver metastases.

title: Physical activity, biological age and DNA methylation clocks
description: Meta-analysis of 44 studies and 145,465 participants: higher physical activity associated with lower Horvath and GrimAge epigenetic age acceleration but not Hannum or PhenoAge.

title: Social organization and lifespan in mammals
description: Reanalysis of 1,436 mammal species: group-living and pair-living species have longer lifespans than solitary species after controlling for body mass and phylogeny.

title: Pulmatrix and Eos SENOLYTIX Announce Merger Agreement
description: Pulmatrix (PULM) acquires Eos SENOLYTIX with $19M concurrent financing to advance MitoXcel peptide platform and lead candidate PTC-2105 for sarcopenia and age-related disease.

title: Methylene Blue May Help Protect Hair Follicle Stem Cells from GLP-1 Metabolic Stress
description: Maryland team shows methylene blue boosts HFSC proliferation via β-catenin activation, synergizes with minoxidil, and protects stem cells from GLP-1 receptor agonist-induced cell death — a mechanistic counter-strategy for semaglutide/tirzepatide hair thinning.

title: Extending Healthspan Through Public Health and Longevity Medicine
description: Editorial argues healthy longevity will depend on coordinated integration of multiple intervention layers — public health, primary prevention, geroscience pharmaceuticals, and individualized longevity medicine.

title: Tozorakimab meets primary endpoint in Phase III MIRANDA COPD trial
description: AstraZeneca's IL-33 monoclonal hit primary endpoint in MIRANDA Phase 3 COPD trial — validating IL-33 blockade as a disease-modifying mechanism for a major disease of aging.

title: U of Arizona launches $12M rapamycin Phase 3 trial in older adults
description: Coit College of Pharmacy 2-year double-blind low-dose rapamycin trial in 65+ adults measuring frailty transitions and IL-6 inflammaging — alongside parallel UT San Antonio NIA trial.

title: Insilico forms Longevity Board to oversee AI aging research
description: Insilico stands up dedicated Longevity Board to oversee its AI-driven aging research program, formalizing geroscience commitment alongside the $2.75B Eli Lilly deal.

title: BioAge reports positive Phase 1 data for BGE-102
description: BioAge Labs reports positive Phase 1 results for BGE-102, NLRP3 inflammasome inhibitor program supporting further cardiometabolic/inflammaging development.

title: Vector Y wins FDA Fast Track for ALS therapy VTX002
description: Amsterdam-based Vector Y receives FDA Fast Track for VTX002 targeting TDP43 — Pioneer ALS Phase 1/2 enrollment begins 2026.

title: FDA clears safety step for Loyal's senior dog longevity drug LOY-002
description: Loyal's LOY-002 cleared FDA safety hurdle for senior dog lifespan extension; FDA noted reasonable expectation of effectiveness from real-world trial of 1,300+ dogs across 400 sites.

title: Corsera lands $80M to predict and prevent cardiovascular disease
description: Corsera closes oversubscribed $80M Series for two-pronged AI lifetime risk assessment plus long-acting RNAi therapy COR-10004 targeting cholesterol, currently in Phase 1.

title: Engitix raises $25M to target tissue aging at its source
description: Engitix raises $25M for extracellular matrix targeting platform spanning fibrosis and solid tumors, leveraging proprietary ECM dataset for earlier-stage intervention.
